_comment: TEAM 2 — THE RULE BOOK. Real citations. Change only if you can point to the source.

co2_ppm.source: ASHRAE Standard 62.1-2022, Appendix D; outdoor CO2 ~420 ppm + 700 ppm differential
co2_ppm.green_meaning: Fresh air, good ventilation. Safe for all occupants.
co2_ppm.red_meaning: Poor air quality. Occupants may feel drowsy. Ventilate immediately.

pm25_ugm3.source: EPA AQI breakpoints for PM2.5 (24-hour average), 40 CFR Part 58 Appendix G
pm25_ugm3.green_meaning: Good air quality. No particulate health risk.
pm25_ugm3.yellow_meaning: Moderate. Sensitive groups (asthma, heart disease) may be affected.
pm25_ugm3.red_meaning: Unhealthy. Keep windows CLOSED during smoke events.
pm25_ugm3.smoke_warning: During a wildfire smoke event, DO NOT open windows. Outdoor air is the problem.

temperature_f.source: OSHA Technical Manual Section III Chapter 2; ASHRAE 55 comfort zone
temperature_f.yellow_meaning: Outside comfort zone. Monitor elderly and children.
temperature_f.red_meaning: Dangerous. Risk of heat stress or hypothermia.

humidity_pct.yellow_meaning: Outside ideal range. Dry air irritates airways; high humidity encourages mold.
humidity_pct.red_meaning: Dangerous. Mold risk above 70%, respiratory irritation below 20%.

voc_index.yellow_meaning: Elevated VOCs. Check for cleaning products or off-gassing.
voc_index.red_meaning: High VOC levels. Ventilate and investigate source.

beps_compliance._what_is_beps: Montgomery County Building Energy Performance Standards — large buildings must cut energy use or pay fines.

penalty.note: Paid every year the building misses the target.

BAS-1_weekend_co2.description: AHU-3/4/5 should be OFF on weekends. 3rd-floor CO2 should approach outdoor baseline (~420 ppm).

BAS-2_setback.description: After 9pm temp should drift toward 67F. If it holds at 72F, setback isn't working.

no_backup_generator.note: Building goes dark in an outage.

r22_chillers.note: Refrigerant phased out — can't be recharged.

aging_ahus_1994.note: AHUs older than most of the staff.

no_air_purifiers.note: Nothing to scrub smoke if PM2.5 spikes.
